title: Emergency Stop / Kill Switch
summary: Defines the exact semantics of a kill command — what is cancelled, what is flattened, what is halted, and in what order. The kill switch is a hard safety requirement before any live capital is deployed.

level_1_halt_signals.description: Block all new scan results from producing orders. Open positions are held. Pending orders are left in place.
level_1_halt_signals.how: Set dash['killed'] = True. Signal dispatcher checks this flag before submitting any order.
level_1_halt_signals.trigger: Not exposed directly — Level 3 always used from external triggers

level_2_cancel_pending.description: Cancel all open (unfilled) orders that have been submitted to IBKR but not yet filled.
level_2_cancel_pending.how: ib.reqGlobalCancel() — cancels all open orders on the active account.
level_2_cancel_pending.trigger: Called as part of Level 3 sequence

level_3_flatten.description: Market-sell all open long positions. Market-buy to close all open short positions. Then cancel pending orders. Then halt signals.
level_3_flatten.how: Iterate active_trades, submit market close order per position, then reqGlobalCancel(), then set dash['killed'] = True.
level_3_flatten.trigger: Default action for all external kill triggers

kill_sequence_order: 1. Submit market close orders for all open positions (flatten)
2. Call ib.reqGlobalCancel() to cancel all pending unfilled orders
3. Set dash['killed'] = True to block new signal-driven orders
4. Log KILL_ACTIVATED event with positions_flattened and orders_cancelled counts
5. Send confirmation back to triggering channel (Telegram or dashboard)

triggers.authorized: localhost only (dashboard is not externally exposed)

resume_path.note: Resume clears the halt flag only. It does NOT re-open flattened positions. Positions must be re-entered by normal signal flow.

status_command.telegram: /status — reports dash['killed'], open position count, current PnL
status_command.dashboard: Status visible on main dashboard header

files: bot.py — dash['killed'] flag, /api/kill route, on_kill/on_status/on_resume callbacks
known_limitations: If IBKR is disconnected when /kill is received, flatten orders cannot be submitted. The halt flag (level_1) is still set. Manual intervention required to close positions.
Market-close orders during pre/post market may not fill at expected prices.
